protocol_definition.termination: Stop after ballot aggregation; deterministic lowest-index tie break.

intermediate_messages.content:
### 1. Executive Summary and Assumptions
This strategic launch plan outlines the development and implementation of a pilot AI course-QA assistant for two undergraduate courses, serving approximately 300 students. The assistant will utilize instructor-approved materials and include safeguards for hallucination, privacy, academic integrity, and escalation to human staff. The project is supported by a part-time faculty sponsor, two student developers, and a teaching assistant, with a total budget of $5,000.

**Assumptions:**
- The AI assistant will only use approved course materials.
- The part-time faculty sponsor will be available for guidance and approvals.
- The student developers have the necessary skills to build the assistant.
- The teaching assistant will support testing and user feedback.
- Privacy and academic integrity are prioritized in the development process.

### 2. Goals and Non-Goals
**Goals:**
- Develop a functional AI course-QA assistant by the end of Week 12.
- Ensure the assistant adheres to privacy and academic integrity standards.
- Gather user feedback from students and faculty for future iterations.

**Non-Goals:**
- The project will not develop a fully autonomous AI system.
- The assistant will not provide answers outside of approved materials.
- The project will not address broader AI ethical concerns beyond the scope of the course.

### 3. Stakeholder and Decision-Rights Map
- **Faculty Sponsor:** Final approval on content and project direction.
- **Student Developers:** Responsible for technical development and implementation.
- **Teaching Assistant:** Facilitates testing, feedback collection, and user support.
- **Students:** End-users providing feedback and usage data.

### 4. Baseline 12-Week Roadmap Table

| Week | Activity                                      | Owner                | Dependencies                | Milestone/Decision Gate                |
|------|-----------------------------------------------|----------------------|-----------------------------|----------------------------------------|
| 1    | Project kickoff and requirements gathering    | Faculty Sponsor       | None                        | Approval of project scope              |
| 2    | Design AI assistant architecture               | Student Developers    | Requirements gathering      | Design approval                        |
| 3    | Develop initial prototype                     | Student Developers    | Design completion           | Prototype ready for testing            |
| 4    | Conduct initial testing with faculty input    | Teaching Assistant     | Prototype development       | Feedback collected                     |
| 5    | Revise prototype based on feedback            | Student Developers    | Testing results             | Revised prototype ready                |
| 6    | Implement privacy safeguards                   | Student Developers    | Revised prototype           | Privacy review completed               |
| 7    | Conduct user testing with students             | Teaching Assistant     | Privacy safeguards          | User feedback collected                |
| 8    | Finalize content approval process              | Faculty Sponsor       | User testing                | Content approved                       |
| 9    | Prepare for launch                            | All                   | Content approval            | Launch readiness review                |
| 10   | Launch AI assistant                           | All                   | Launch preparation          | Assistant live for students            |
| 11   | Monitor usage and gather feedback              | Teaching Assistant     | Launch                      | Initial feedback report                |
| 12   | Final evaluation and reporting                 | Faculty Sponsor       | Feedback collection         | Project evaluation completed           |

### 5. Baseline Resource and Budget Allocation

| Resource                | Allocation         |
|------------------------|--------------------|
| Faculty Sponsor         | Part-time (10 hrs/week) |
| Student Developers      | 2 x Part-time (20 hrs/week each) |
| Teaching Assistant      | Part-time (15 hrs/week) |
| Budget                  | $5,000              |
| - Development           | $2,500              |
| - Infrastructure        | $1,000              |
| - Privacy Review        | $1,000              |
| - Testing               | $500                |

### 6. Week 6-8 Contingency Overlay and Ownership Changes
With one student developer unavailable during Weeks 6-8, the remaining developer will take on additional responsibilities, focusing on privacy safeguards and user testing. The teaching assistant will assist in development tasks to mitigate the impact of reduced capacity.

### 7. Revised Budget Showing the Exact $750 Reallocation

| Resource                | Revised Allocation  |
|------------------------|---------------------|
| Development            | $2,500              |
| Infrastructure         | $250                |
| Privacy Review         | $1,750              |
| Testing                | $500                |

### 8. Risk Register and Mitigations

| Risk                          | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation Strategy                          |
|-------------------------------|------------|--------|---------------------------------------------|
| Developer unavailability       | Medium     | High   | Cross-train team members on key tasks      |
| Privacy concerns               | Medium     | High   | Conduct thorough privacy review and testing |
| Inadequate user feedback       | Low        | Medium | Engage students early in the testing phase |
| Budget overruns                | Low        | High   | Monitor expenses closely and adjust as needed |

### 9. Success Metrics and Data-Collection Plan
- **User Engagement:** Track the number of interactions with the assistant.
- **Feedback Quality:** Collect qualitative feedback from students and faculty.
- **Accuracy of Responses:** Measure the percentage of correct answers provided by the assistant.
- **Privacy Compliance:** Ensure all safeguards are functioning as intended.

### 10. Governance, Content Approval, and Escalation Process
- **Governance:** Weekly check-ins with the faculty sponsor to review progress.
- **Content Approval:** All materials used by the assistant must be pre-approved by the faculty sponsor.
- **Escalation Process:** Any issues or concerns will be escalated to the faculty sponsor for resolution.

### 11. Expand / Revise-Extend / Stop Decision Criteria
- **Expand:** If user engagement exceeds 75% and feedback is overwhelmingly positive, consider expanding the assistant's capabilities.
- **Revise-Extend:** If user feedback indicates significant issues but potential, revise the assistant and extend the pilot for an additional 4 weeks.
- **Stop:** If the assistant fails to meet privacy standards or receives negative feedback from over 50% of users, halt the project.

### 12. Final Recommendation
Proceed with the pilot AI course-QA assistant as outlined in this strategic launch plan. Ensure adherence to privacy and academic integrity standards while actively engaging stakeholders throughout the process. Adjust the project scope and resources as necessary to accommodate the identified contingency.

This strategic launch plan outlines a 12-week pilot for an AI course-QA assistant designed to support two undergraduate courses with approximately 300 students. - Develop a functional AI course-QA assistant by the end of Week 12.

- Achieve a user satisfaction rate of at least 75% from student feedback.

**Non-Goals:**
- The assistant will not provide answers outside of approved course materials.
- The project will not include extensive marketing or outreach beyond the two courses.

### 3. Stakeholder and Decision-Rights Map
- **Faculty Sponsor:** Final approval on content and project direction.
- **Student Developers:** Responsible for technical development and implementation.
- **Teaching Assistant:** Liaison between students and developers, responsible for gathering feedback.

### 4. Baseline 12-Week Roadmap Table

| Week | Activity                                      | Owner                | Dependencies                  | Milestone/Decision Gate          |
|------|-----------------------------------------------|----------------------|-------------------------------|----------------------------------|

| 9    | Collect user feedback                          | Teaching Assistant    | Pilot launch                  | Feedback collection              |
| 10   | Analyze feedback and make adjustments          | Student Developers    | Feedback collection           | Adjusted assistant               |
| 11   | Prepare final report and recommendations       | Faculty Sponsor       | Adjusted assistant            | Final report completion          |
| 12   | Present findings and gather final feedback     | Teaching Assistant    | Final report                  | Project closure                  |

### 5. Baseline Resource and Budget Allocation

| Resource                | Allocation         |
|------------------------|--------------------|
| Faculty Sponsor         | Part-time          |
| Student Developers      | 2 x Part-time      |
| Teaching Assistant      | Part-time          |
| Budget                  | $5,000             |
| Infrastructure          | $1,500             |
| Development Tools       | $1,000             |
| Privacy Review          | $750               |
| Testing and Feedback    | $1,000             |
| Contingency Fund        | $750               |
| Miscellaneous           | $1,000             |

### 6. Week 6-8 Contingency Overlay and Ownership Changes
Due to the unavailability of one student developer during Weeks 6-8, the following changes will occur:
- The remaining student developer will take on additional responsibilities, focusing on privacy review and testing.
- The teaching assistant will assist in the development process to ensure timelines are met.

### 7. Revised Budget Showing the Exact $750 Reallocation

| Resource                | Allocation         |
|------------------------|--------------------|
| Faculty Sponsor         | Part-time          |
| Student Developers      | 1 x Part-time      |
| Teaching Assistant      | Part-time          |
| Budget                  | $5,000             |
| Infrastructure          | $750 (reduced)     |
| Development Tools       | $1,000             |
| Privacy Review          | $1,500 (increased) |
| Testing and Feedback    | $1,000             |
| Contingency Fund        | $750               |
| Miscellaneous           | $1,000             |

### 8. Risk Register and Mitigations

| Risk                          | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation Strategy                          |
|-------------------------------|------------|--------|---------------------------------------------|
| Developer unavailability       | Medium     | High   | Cross-train team members on key tasks.     |
| Privacy compliance failure     | Low        | High   | Conduct thorough reviews and testing.       |
| Student dissatisfaction        | Medium     | Medium | Regular feedback collection and adjustments. |
| Budget overruns                | Low        | High   | Monitor expenses closely and adjust as needed.|

### 9. Success Metrics and Data-Collection Plan
- **User Satisfaction Rate:** Target 75% satisfaction from student feedback.
- **Usage Metrics:** Track the number of interactions with the assistant.
- **Feedback Quality:** Analyze qualitative feedback for improvement areas.
- **Privacy Compliance:** Ensure all safeguards are met through testing.

### 10. Governance, Content Approval, and Escalation Process
- **Governance:** The faculty sponsor will oversee the project and approve all content.
- **Content Approval:** All materials used by the assistant must be pre-approved by the faculty sponsor.
- **Escalation Process:** Any issues or concerns raised by students will be escalated to the teaching assistant, who will communicate with the faculty sponsor.

### 11. Expand / Revise-Extend / Stop Decision Criteria
- **Expand:** If user satisfaction exceeds 85% and usage metrics are high, consider expanding the assistant to additional courses.
- **Revise-Extend:** If satisfaction is between 60-85%, revise the assistant based on feedback and extend the pilot for an additional 4 weeks.
- **Stop:** If satisfaction is below 60% or significant compliance issues arise, halt the project and reassess.

### 12. Final Recommendation
Proceed with the pilot AI course-QA assistant as outlined in this strategic launch plan. Ensure adherence to privacy and academic integrity standards while actively collecting feedback for continuous improvement. Adjust the project scope and resources as necessary to accommodate the identified contingencies.
